behaviors.description: Conversation history is trimmed to the most recent 10 messages before the LLM call when history is too long.
behaviors.justification: BOUNDARY-PROOF: Unit -> Integration because the failure is at a handoff boundary: history-trimming logic must be correctly applied in the request builder that passes payload to the LLM client (a cross-module contract, not just one function). INTEGRATION-BRAKE: no real OS/network/subprocess behavior is required; no AI-output-quality dependency; no multi-agentic chain. REJECTION-GATE: rejected System/Agentic/Workflow because behavior text is about 'history passed to the LLM is trimmed' (payload construction), not real transport conditions or model output quality.
behaviors.test_description: Wire conversation service + prompt builder + LLM client adapter (stub). Feed >10 prior messages and assert the outbound LLM request contains exactly the most recent 10 messages, in order.

behaviors.description: Generated response answers the customer question instead of being generic or off-topic.
behaviors.justification: BOUNDARY-PROOF: Unit -> Integration because generation path combines prompt assembly + LLM invocation + output handling. Integration -> System because this behavior requires a real model/API call path, not just in-process stubs, to expose true response behavior. System -> Agentic because correctness is semantic relevance ('addresses the question') and depends on real model output quality/non-determinism. REJECTION-GATE: rejected Workflow because behavior text describes a single response quality outcome, not multiple agentic steps in sequence.
behaviors.test_description: Run real prompts against the production-like model endpoint over a labeled set of support questions; score whether each answer is on-topic and question-addressing (human rubric or adjudicator), and fail below threshold.

behaviors.description: Tone re-check detects an overly formal response for a casual-tier customer and triggers regeneration.
behaviors.justification: BOUNDARY-PROOF: Unit -> Integration because failure can occur at the interface between tone-check result and regeneration orchestration. Integration -> System because the tone decision path uses real model/API behavior in practice, not only local code. System -> Agentic because 'correctly identifies ... too formal' is output-quality judgment dependent on model behavior. REJECTION-GATE: rejected Workflow because this behavior requires one model-dependent classification decision plus a deterministic trigger assertion; it does not require validating multiple agentic outputs end-to-end.
behaviors.test_description: Use real model-based tone check with casual-tier fixtures and intentionally formal candidate replies; assert the system flags them and issues a regeneration request.

behaviors.description: User message, initial response, and any regenerations are persisted with correct timestamps.
behaviors.justification: BOUNDARY-PROOF: Unit -> Integration because this is a multi-module data persistence contract (chat orchestration -> persistence layer -> DB schema). INTEGRATION-BRAKE: no required real network/OS primitive beyond local DB wiring; no model-quality dependency; no multi-agentic requirement for timestamp correctness. REJECTION-GATE: rejected System because behavior text is 'persisted to the database with correct timestamps' and can be caught with an in-process/local test DB transactionally.
behaviors.test_description: Run chat flow with one regeneration using a test database; assert all exchange records exist (including rejected draft), foreign-key/session linkage is correct, and timestamps are correctly ordered and populated.

behaviors.description: If tone-triggered regeneration occurs, only the second response is delivered to the customer; rejected draft is never exposed.
behaviors.justification: BOUNDARY-PROOF: Unit -> Integration because this failure appears at module boundaries between generation, tone-check orchestration, and response-delivery adapter. INTEGRATION-BRAKE: no mandatory real OS/network/subprocess condition in behavior text; no dependence on AI quality (can force deterministic first/second outputs); no multi-agentic quality chain required. REJECTION-GATE: rejected System/Agentic/Workflow because behavior text is delivery selection ('second response replaces the first'), which is orchestration/state handling, not model-quality evaluation.
behaviors.test_description: Wire orchestrator + delivery adapter with deterministic LLM stubs (first draft flagged, second approved). Assert outbound customer payload contains only the regenerated response and first draft is absent from delivery channel.
